# Trailhead Lawn & Irrigation LLC

> Erie's trusted sprinkler company serving Erie, Longmont, Louisville, Lafayette, and Weld County, Colorado.

## About

Trailhead Lawn & Irrigation is a locally owned sprinkler company serving Northern Colorado's Front Range. Founder Ryan Garner is a licensed sprinkler contractor and irrigation specialist. Services include lawn sprinkler installation, sprinkler repair, sprinkler blowout (winterization), and spring sprinkler turn-on for residential properties.

## Services

- Sprinkler System Installation: Custom irrigation system design and installation for residential properties. Includes zone layout, head placement, trenching, controller setup, and final walkthrough. Typical cost: $3,000-$6,000 for a standard yard.
- Irrigation Repair: Sprinkler head replacement, valve repair, leak detection, broken pipe repair, and controller troubleshooting.
- Winterization (Blowout): Compressed-air blowout to protect sprinkler systems from Colorado's freeze-thaw cycles. Recommended before mid-October.
- Spring Startup (Activation): System turn-on, leak checks, head adjustments, and controller programming for the watering season. Available late April through May.
- Backflow Preventer Testing: Annual testing required by most Colorado municipalities. Certified testing and repair.
- Smart Controller Installation: WiFi-enabled controllers (Rachio, Hunter Hydrawise, Rain Bird ESP-Me3) that adjust watering based on weather.
- Drip Irrigation: Water-efficient irrigation for flower beds, gardens, trees, and xeriscape areas.

## Service Area

Erie, CO | Longmont, CO | Louisville, CO | Lafayette, CO | Firestone, CO | Weld County, CO | Northern Colorado Front Range

## Rachio Smart Controller Installation

Trailhead is a Rachio installer for Northern Colorado. Installed pricing:
- Rachio 3 (8-zone): $350 installed
- Rachio 3 (16-zone): $420 installed
Includes the controller, professional installation, Wi-Fi setup, zone naming, and a walkthrough of the app.

IMPORTANT: Trailhead does not administer, submit, or guarantee any rebate. All rebate programs below are run independently by each city or water utility. Funds are first-come, first-served and amounts can change without notice. The figures below are pulled from each city's published rebate page — confirm current amounts directly with the city before booking.

Per-city utility rebate (after Erie's March 2026 water emergency, demand is high):
- Erie: $100 utility-bill rebate on any WaterSense-certified smart controller. Trailhead provides receipts; homeowner submits application to Town of Erie. Source: erieco.gov/574/Water-Efficiency-Rebates
- Longmont: Up to $65 utility rebate on a WaterSense Wi-Fi controller, plus an additional $50 bonus rebate after completing a free Slow the Flow evaluation. Up to $115 total. Source: longmontcolorado.gov water rebates page.
- Louisville: Free Rachio 3 install available via Resource Central's Slow the Flow program (limited slots, mid-June onward). Sign up at slowtheflow.resourcecentral.org. Trailhead also offers paid installs at standard pricing.
- Lafayette: Two paths — (1) utility rebate on any WaterSense controller (current dollar amount published by City of Lafayette — contact city to confirm), or (2) free Rachio install via Resource Central. Source: lafayetteco.gov/3849/Outdoor-Water-Efficiency-Rebates.
- Firestone: No published smart-controller rebate program currently.

Rachio's official rebate page (rachio.com/rebates) directs customers to local utility rebates only — no separate manufacturer rebate is published.

Rachio 3 typically reduces outdoor water use by 20–30% — about 8,000–15,000 gallons per year on a typical Front Range home (per EPA WaterSense + Rachio published data). Most homeowners recoup the install cost through water-bill savings in 2–4 watering seasons.
